summarization.a: In-plane 2θχ XRD of 100cLi-MnO₂, 200cLi-MnO₂, and 300cLi-MnO₂. Li₁₋ₓMn₂O₄ spinel reflections (111), (131), (440) dominate; β-MnO₂ peaks are absent. Asterisk marks Li₂₋ᵤMnO₂ impurity (~47°). Inset shows measurement geometry.
summarization.b: Out-of-plane θ-2θ XRD of the same samples. The 100cLi-MnO₂ sample still shows weak β-MnO₂ (200), but in 200cLi and 300cLi samples only the lithiated phase remains. Peaks shift to lower 2θ with increasing Li content.

a.question: What happens to the β-MnO₂ phase when lithium concentration exceeds x ≈ 0.5?
a.answer: The β-MnO₂ reflections disappear completely, indicating full transformation to the Li₁₋ₓMn₂O₄ spinel phase

a.question: What does the asterisk (*) in panel (a) indicate?
a.answer: An impurity reflection at ~47.4°, attributed to a lithium-rich Li₂₋ᵤMnO₂ phase.

b.question: Why do the lithiated phase peaks shift to lower 2θ angles with increasing lithium content?
b.answer: The lattice expands as more Li⁺ is incorporated, increasing d-spacing and shifting reflections to lower angles according to Bragg's law.

b.question: Can the out-of-plane reflections be indexed to cubic Li₁₋ₓMn₂O₄?
b.answer: Not directly. The d-spacings show integer relationships suggesting multiples of a single reflection, but they don't match cubic spinel indexing. The authors propose that stress and microstrain distort the cubic structure in one dimension, breaking Fd3̄m symmetry.
